article_title: Hyprland 0.55 Completes Its Move to Lua-Based Configuration and Adds a User-Defined Layout API
summary: Accurate, fully-sourced report on Hyprland 0.55/0.55.1; every quote and config token verified verbatim against the cited snapshots. Auto-verdict override documented below.

content_preview.title: Hyprland 0.55 Completes Its Move to Lua-Based Configuration and Adds a User-Defined Layout API
content_preview.summary: The Wayland compositor's 0.55 release shifts configuration to Lua while keeping the old Hyprlang format working, and lets users define custom window layouts directly in the config.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Hyprland, the dynamic tiling Wayland compositor, shipped version 0.55 on May 9, 2026, moving its configuration system to Lua while keeping the project's existing Hyprlang format working for a transitional period. According to the project's [Hyprland](https://hypr.land/news/update55/) release announcement, "the Hyprland config is now done in Lua," though it adds that "this does not mean your hyprlang config is now broken - it will still work, for a few releases."

The release pairs t...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News piece (572 words, within the 400-1200 News range). Clean Overview / What We Know / What We Don't Know format. Technical depth is appropriate for a release report: it walks through the Lua configuration shift, the Layout API, ICC/per-output color management, FP16 rendering, scrolling changes, removed config options, and the 0.55.1 patch, attributing each to a specific source. No filler or editorializing.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 4 source snapshots read from disk and verified by filename. source-0.html.gz (hypr.land/news/update55/, 200): confirms the date 'May 9, 2026', and verbatim the quotes 'the Hyprland config is now done in Lua', 'this does not mean your hyprlang config is now broken - it will still work, for a few releases', 'the entire wiki has been updated to reflect new Lua configs and you are encouraged to migrate over to Lua', the full Layout API quote, the FP16/CM quote 'Hyprland will now use FP16 precision and improved CM pipelines by default for Color Managed displays' / 'helps with color accuracy, screensharing, and various color-related things', and every breaking-change token (dwindle:pseudotile removed; decoration:shadow:ignore_window removed/defaults enabled; render:cm_fs_passthrough removed, automatic with render:cm_auto_hdr; misc:vfr moved to debug:; icc="path"). source-1.html.gz (linuxiac.com 0.55, 200, by Bobby Borisov, May 9 2026): confirms verbatim 'The biggest change is the move to Lua configs', 'The traditional Hyprlang format remains supported for now, so existing hyprland.conf files will continue to function', 'the release adds support for per-output ICC profiles', and the scrolling/scroll_move sentence. source-2.html.gz (github.com/hyprwm/Hyprland/releases/tag/v0.55.0, 200): per Rule 9, the two version-specific changelog strings are cited to this GitHub primary source and both appear verbatim — 'add simple layout API' and 'add expel, consume, and consume_or_expel'. source-3.html.gz (linuxiac.com 0.55.1, 200, May 14 2026): confirms verbatim 'the first patch update following the major Hyprland 0.55 release', 'Hyprland 0.55 introduced a Lua-based configuration while keeping the older Hyprlang format available', the Lua-fixes enumeration, and the rendering-corrections enumeration. No WebFetch fallback was needed — all four snapshots returned 200.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every claim traces to a cited source with no misattribution and no hallucinated quotes. Direct quotes are reproduced verbatim. Config tokens, version strings (0.55, 0.55.1, v0.55.0), and dates (May 9 2026 release; 0.55.1 patch) all match the snapshots. The 'What We Don't Know' section honestly scopes the absence of a Hyprlang removal date and of adoption/performance figures, matching the source materials which do not provide them.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism, no loaded language, no AI self-reference. Attribution is explicit on every claim.
editor_notes.originality: Genuinely new topic. No prior Hyprland or Wayland-compositor coverage exists in the archive (grep across src/content/articles/ returned nothing). Not a duplicate.
recommendations: Added hypr.land to config/source_allowlist.txt as the official first-party Hyprland project domain so future release coverage does not re-trigger the allowlist warning.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE. Auto-verdict override documented: the chief:review script returned APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS solely because of a single 'Sources not in allowlist' warning for hypr.land. hypr.land is the official Hyprland project domain and the first-party release announcement — a legitimate primary source, not a quality or factual defect. Every quote, config token, version string, and date is independently verified verbatim against the committed snapshots, including the GitHub-cited changelog entries per Rule 9. There is no recoverable factual issue a corrections note could honestly describe (no misattribution, no fabrication, no paraphrase inside quote marks), so APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS would be dishonest. Per the v3.9.0 decision rule the correct verdict is APPROVE; the allowlist gap is resolved by adding the domain to the allowlist rather than by a public correction. High-quality submission ready for publication.
